howItWorks.intro: This osha fine calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Base Penalty = Maximum for Violation Type x Severity Multiplier
Final Penalty = Base x (1 - Size Reduction - Good Faith Reduction)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What are 2024 OSHA maximum fines?
faq.answer: Serious/Other: up to $16,131 per violation. Willful/Repeat: up to $161,323 per violation. Failure to Abate: $16,131 per day. Penalties are adjusted annually for inflation. Multiple violations at one worksite can result in millions in total fines.

faq.question: How can OSHA penalties be reduced?
faq.answer: Reductions for: small business size (up to 60% for under 25 employees), good faith/safety programs (up to 25%), and compliance history (up to 10%). Contesting citations through an informal conference can also reduce penalties.

howWeCalculate.intro: This osha fine calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
